content:
# Asphaltene

**Summary**: Heavy, polar molecules in crude oil that can deposit on rock surfaces and alter wettibility.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-al-garadi|AL-GARADI, 2019]] investigated the role of asphaltenes in altering carbonate rock wettibility.

- [[2021-alqam|ALQAM, 2021]] studied the role of asphaltene structure on the wettability of carbonate rocks.

- [[2021-aslanov|Aslanov, 2021]] used asphaltene A1 to represent the bitumen component of organic matter.

## Scientific Context

- [[2019-al-garadi|AL-GARADI, 2019]] Asphaltenes are a primary mechanism for altering carbonate reservoir wettibility during aging.

- [[2021-alqam|ALQAM, 2021]] studied the role of asphaltene structure on the wettability of carbonate rocks.

- [[2021-aslanov|Aslanov, 2021]] used asphaltene A1 to represent the bitumen component of organic matter.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2019-al-garadi|AL-GARADI, 2019]] Comparison of samples with different asphaltene contents.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Asphaltene content is a significant crude oil component causing wettibility alteration to intermediate or oil-wet. ([[2019-al-garadi|AL-GARADI, 2019, p. 31]])


- Asphaltenes were found to have condensed aromatic cores of 8-9 rings with varying sulfur content. ([[2021-alqam|ALQAM, 2021, p. 101]])

- Asphaltene was more prone to elongate in the direction perpendicular to the force, resulting in a higher Poisson's ratio (0.47) compared to kerogen (0.42). ([[2021-aslanov|Aslanov, 2021, p. 56]])
